review_text: Beautiful Campground on the Thredbo river
Beautiful camp ground.  Neat and tidy. Local deer and ducks wondering freely, with the beautiful sound of the running river.
Absolutely love this place camp here every time we come from Brisbane 👌😍 night times are awesome there

Lovely camping spot, fire pits running river & toilets close by, rangers checked in regularly they were great & friendly.
Bring your own firewood.
Had the best time camping in the snow ⛄️
Pretty good.. 2 clean toilets
Great spot to access Thredbo, been here multiple times in summer and love it

Nice location with lots of wildlife and close to local tracks and the Thredbo village, with good amenities. Overall great experience, would recommend coming here with friends. We met some great ducks in the lovely, tranquil river. It really highlighted my experience.
Cracking location with hikes and river on your doorstep. Sites are smaller but suitable and starlink reception was good.
Excellent service and hospitality

Ngarigo was a favourite paid campground of ours. Located within Mt Kosciuszko it was peaceful and a great base to visit Threadbo.

The bush toilet was clean and we enjoyed our few days. There are well signed walking/biking tracks from the campground.
Great campground on the banks of the Thredbo River.  Only a short drive to Thredbo Village where an endless  amount of walks and mountain bike trails are waiting for you to enjoy.
Nice camp ground.
Good spot to camp if you want to be close to thredbo. Be sure to put your rubbish away at night, possums will go through it. Also saw some deer in the morning.

Beautiful campground.

Wild ducks, deer and possums dwell here and the ducks might get close to your camp

It gets dark very quickly. Good for stargazing.

Only 6$.

Lots of space around - generally beautiful.

In autumn and winter it's pretty much -1 degrees at night so bring warm clothes. The fog sets down sometimes and when you wake up, there will be nice frosty grass.

Nice fire pits. 👍
Great campground just next to the river. It has designated camp spots.  Most of them have picking tables and fire pits.

Unsafe camping area as bikes rode through it all day and night. Felt very unsafe. Also, many many ticks in grass.
A carpark in front of a cycle route and toilet is not appropriate, perhaps a fence should be installed so one is not camping in the main traffic way
Beautiful camping ground by the river. Ideallyic if you can get it when not too many other people.
This is a great campground. It's just a shame that it rained pretty much the whole time I was there 🥺
Good spot to enjoy the area as its best ! All you need and clean, quiet to get a good rest. Kangooroos all around, loved it !
To camp one needs to book it which was introduced three years ago to manage COVID crowds.
Beautiful camp grounds flat and sunny by the river

This campsite 🏕️ is great.
It doesn’t have any facilities apart from bathrooms.

Such a gem of a campsite (vans, tents, rooftops) only 8 klms from Thredbo.
Nice clean facilities, good fire pits.
Definitely gets cold we had -9 and all our water in tanks and pipes froze.
Would definitely stay again, sites 1 -10 were in higher ground.
Loud mouth yobbo’s up till all hours, would never stay here again

Decent campground. Various sites and different sizes for vans and tents. We stayed for the snow at Thredbo so very close. We had van site 26 and it was quite muddy. Other sites were higher and stayed dry. Beware that generators are allowed and our lovely neighbour felt it was ok to start his bloody loud generator at 6am. When we asked why, he said he had a right to and he needed to warm his van up so he could get ready to go up out. 🙄
He was very inconsiderate and rude.
We asked the ranger about noise restrictions and he thought 6am was totally fine. 😕 so we packed up and left early. Most people were quiet though.
